repo_notes.content: do not talk about the claude code codebase inspiration/parity part of the codebase, mention syndicate mode all over where relevant and give it a section, have a visionary/innovative tone in the overview.

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to Eidolon CLI — what it is, why it exists, the Regula Framework philosophy, and how the two major workspaces (Rust implementation and Python reference) relate. Links to all major child sections.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for building from source, authenticating (API key and OAuth PKCE), running the first REPL session, and using the /doctor command to validate the environment. Covers prerequisites, container workflow, and project initialization.

pages.title: Usage Guide
pages.purpose: Comprehensive reference for all running modes (REPL, one-shot prompt, JSON output), CLI flags, model aliases, slash commands, session management, and Syndicate mode invocation from the command line.

pages.title: Rust Workspace Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of the eight-crate Rust workspace structure, the layered dependency model, and how the binary crate (eidolon-cli) sits atop the library crates. Links to child pages for each major subsystem.

pages.title: eidolon-cli Binary Crate
pages.purpose: Deep dive into the CLI binary: argument parsing, REPL loop, TUI rendering with crossterm/rustyline/syntect, slash command dispatch, session I/O, OAuth flow, and the CliAction dispatch table (Repl, Prompt, Login, Doctor, Syndicate).

pages.title: Runtime Crate — Core Engine
pages.purpose: Detailed coverage of the runtime crate: conversation loop, config resolution and merging, session persistence (JSONL), prompt assembly, permission enforcement, sandbox management, OAuth, SSE streaming, and the full module inventory of runtime/src/.

pages.title: Permission and Policy System
pages.purpose: Explains the three permission modes (read-only, workspace-write, danger-full-access), the PermissionEnforcer gatekeeper, the policy engine, trust resolver, and how tool calls are validated before execution.

pages.title: Session Management and Compaction
pages.purpose: Covers JSONL session storage, session naming conventions, session resumption (--resume latest), token-aware compaction via compact_session(), summary compression, and the session_control module.

pages.title: MCP Integration
pages.purpose: Explains the Model Context Protocol subsystem: McpServerManager lifecycle, transport types (stdio, websocket, remote, managed_proxy), mcp_tool_bridge translation to GlobalToolRegistry, McpLifecycleValidator, and the mcp_client transport implementations.

pages.title: Syndicate Multi-Agent Orchestration
pages.purpose: Covers the Syndicate subsystem: collection discovery, SyndicateOrchestrator, shared SyndicateMemory (Write/Read/Log/Search), spawn safety guards (session spawn cap, recursion protection), task packets, task registry, team/cron registries, and worker boot.

pages.title: API Crate — LLM Provider Abstraction
pages.purpose: Documents the api crate: the Provider trait, AnthropicClient, OpenAI-compatible client, SSE streaming, prompt caching, error types, and how detect_provider_kind() selects the backend. Covers the three supported providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI Grok).

pages.title: Tools Crate — Tool Registry and Execution
pages.purpose: Explains the GlobalToolRegistry, ToolSpec definitions for all built-in tools (bash, read_file, write_file, edit_file, glob_search, grep_search, Agent, Skill, Syndicate memory tools, etc.), permission-gated dispatch via execute_tool_with_enforcer(), and lane completion.

pages.title: Commands Crate — Slash Command Registry
pages.purpose: Documents the slash command system: slash_command_specs() declarative registry, SlashCommandSpec structure, dispatch_slash_command(), and the full list of built-in slash commands (/doctor, /status, /model, /permissions, /syndicate, /mcp, /skills, /plugins, etc.).

pages.title: Plugins Crate — Hook System
pages.purpose: Covers the plugin architecture: plugin kinds (builtin, bundled, external), plugin.json manifest format, hook events (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure), HookRunner execution, plugin lifecycle (discovery, healthcheck, registration), and bundled example plugins.

pages.title: Telemetry Crate
pages.purpose: Documents the telemetry crate: ClientIdentity, AnthropicRequestProfile, request tracing headers (anthropic-version, beta flags), analytics event logging to JSONL, and how telemetry integrates with the conversation loop.

pages.title: Mock Anthropic Service — Testing Harness
pages.purpose: Explains the mock-anthropic-service crate used for integration testing: how it reads mock_parity_scenarios.json to serve deterministic LLM responses, the mock parity harness test pattern, and how it replaces real API calls in the test suite.

pages.title: Configuration System
pages.purpose: Overview of the layered JSON configuration system, environment variables, and the project directory structure. Links to child pages for the config hierarchy and MCP server configuration.

pages.title: Configuration Hierarchy and Schema
pages.purpose: Details the five-level config merge order (~/.eidolon.json → XDG → project .eidolon.json → .eidolon/settings.json → .eidolon/settings.local.json), all configuration keys (model, permissions, tools, mcpServers), environment variable overrides, and the /config REPL command.

pages.title: Project Directory Structure and Instruction Files
pages.purpose: Documents the .eidolon/ project directory layout: sessions/, skills/, plugins/installed/, instructions.md, settings.local.json. Explains how CLAUDE.md, .eidolon/instructions.md, and ~/.eidolon/instructions.md are aggregated into the system prompt.

pages.title: Python Porting Workspace
pages.purpose: Overview of the src/ Python reference workspace — its role as a structural mirror of the original TypeScript Claude Code codebase, how it relates to the Rust implementation, and the parity audit mechanism. Links to child pages for the workspace internals.

pages.title: Workspace Structure and CLI Entrypoint
pages.purpose: Documents src/main.py as the Python workspace CLI, all subcommands (summary, manifest, parity-audit, commands, tools, route, bootstrap, turn-loop, etc.), the PortManifest, PortRuntime, and QueryEnginePort classes, and how the workspace is organized.

pages.title: Reference Data and Parity Audit
pages.purpose: Explains the reference_data/ directory: commands_snapshot.json, tools_snapshot.json, archive_surface_snapshot.json, and the 29 subsystem JSON files. Documents the parity_audit.py module and how it measures coverage against the archived TypeScript snapshot.

pages.title: Subsystem Placeholder Packages
pages.purpose: Explains the pattern of Python placeholder packages (assistant, bridge, buddy, cli, coordinator, etc.) that mirror TypeScript subsystem directories, how _archive_helper.py loads metadata from reference_data/subsystems/*.json, and what MODULE_COUNT/SAMPLE_FILES expose.

pages.title: Development Guide
pages.purpose: Overview of contributing to Eidolon: workspace standards, testing strategy, and extension patterns. Links to child pages for adding tools/commands/providers and the testing infrastructure.

pages.title: Adding Tools, Commands, and Providers
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for extending Eidolon: implementing a new ToolSpec and registering it in mvp_tool_specs(), adding a SlashCommandSpec to slash_command_specs(), implementing the Provider trait for a new LLM backend, and creating a plugin with a plugin.json manifest.

pages.title: Testing Infrastructure
pages.purpose: Documents the test strategy: mock_parity_harness.rs and mock_parity_scenarios.json for integration tests, cli_flags_and_config_defaults.rs, output_format_contract.rs, resume_slash_commands.rs, the env_lock() mutex for environment variable safety, normalize_path_sep() for cross-platform tests, and the Python test suite in tests/test_porting_workspace.py.

pages.title: Code Standards and Build System
pages.purpose: Covers workspace-wide Rust standards: #![forbid(unsafe_code)], clippy pedantic lints, cargo fmt enforcement, error handling conventions (ApiError, RuntimeError vs String tool errors), the Containerfile-based dev environment, and the rust/scripts/ helper scripts.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Definitions of all codebase-specific terms, jargon, abbreviations, and domain concepts that an onboarding engineer needs to know, with code pointers to relevant files and types.
